resolution_strategies.change_operations: 1. Move the existing activity "notify customer" from the credit-card branch, where it currently follows "pay by credit card", to the outer sequence immediately after the parallel block and before "send goods".

compliance_degree.reason: After the move, every execution of "pack goods" completes inside the parallel block before the process continues to "notify customer". Therefore, "notify customer" always eventually occurs after "pack goods".

resolution_context_consistency.reason: There are no explicit resolution context requirements to preserve, and the change does not introduce obvious conflicts with the existing process behavior.

change_cost.reason: Only one existing activity is relocated. No new control-flow construct is introduced.

structural_quality_impact.reason: The resulting structure remains simple and avoids duplicate notifications. The notification is placed at a clear synchronization point after payment and packing have completed.

change_location.reason: The change is applied around the parallel payment-and-shipping block, which is in the middle of the process between checkout and goods dispatch.

resolution_strategies.change_operations: 1. Insert a new activity "notify customer" directly after "pack goods" inside the shipping branch sequence.

compliance_degree.reason: Because "notify customer" is placed directly after "pack goods" in the same sequence branch, every occurrence of "pack goods" is followed by a notification.

resolution_context_consistency.reason: No existing context requirements are violated. The existing credit-card notification remains unchanged.

change_cost.reason: The strategy requires only a single insertion local to the activity involved in the violation.

structural_quality_impact.reason: Although structurally simple, this strategy may create duplicate notifications for credit-card payments because "notify customer" would occur both in the credit-card branch and after "pack goods".

change_location.reason: The insertion is made inside the shipping branch of the central parallel block.

resolution_strategies.change_operations: 1. Insert a new activity "notify customer" in the outer sequence immediately after the parallel block and before "send goods". 2. Keep the existing "notify customer" activity in the credit-card payment branch unchanged.

compliance_degree.reason: The newly inserted notification after the parallel join guarantees that every execution of "pack goods" is followed by "notify customer", regardless of the selected payment branch.

resolution_context_consistency.reason: The existing behavior of notifying credit-card customers is preserved, and no explicit context requirement is affected.

change_cost.reason: Only one activity is inserted, and no existing activity needs to be moved or deleted.

structural_quality_impact.reason: The process remains structurally valid, but credit-card cases may receive two notifications, which can introduce behavioral redundancy.

change_location.reason: The new activity is inserted immediately after the central parallel block.

resolution_strategies.change_operations: 1. Replace the current parallel block with a sequence containing the existing payment exclusive choice followed by the existing shipping sequence. 2. Move the existing "notify customer" activity from the credit-card branch to immediately after "pack goods" in the new sequence.

compliance_degree.reason: Once the payment and shipping parts are sequentialized and "notify customer" is placed after "pack goods", every execution of "pack goods" is followed by the notification.

resolution_context_consistency.reason: No explicit context requirement is violated, but the existing parallel behavior between payment and shipping is removed, which may affect intended process performance.

change_cost.reason: This strategy requires restructuring the central control-flow construct and relocating an activity.

structural_quality_impact.reason: The process remains structured, but the change significantly alters concurrency by converting parallel execution into sequential execution.

change_location.reason: The restructuring is applied to the central payment-and-shipping block.

best_resolution_reason: RS1 is recommended because it fully restores compliance while avoiding duplicate customer notifications. It requires only a localized move of an existing activity, preserves a clean structured process, and places the notification at the synchronization point after both payment handling and packing have completed.
